title: Mildly Buttery Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A buttery and velvety Algerian dish featuring lamb, white wine vinegar, and a rich blend of spices, perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned lamb from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the white wine vinegar to the pot and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the browned lamb back to the pot and pour in the chicken broth.
Cover the pot and bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the unsalted butter until it is melted and the sauce is smooth and velvety.
